p1Cap: A skill you can't picture is a skill you can't finish.
fSkillHint: Be specific. Not “guitar” but “play 5 songs people recognize.”
fWhyHint: The reason you'll come back on a tired Tuesday.
fWhyPh: What changes for you once you can do this?
fGoodHint: Your finish line — a concrete thing you can do, not a feeling.
fGoodPh: I'll know I'm there when I can…
fHoursHint: 20 hours gets most skills past the painful-beginner wall. Adjust to ambition.
p2Cap: Big skills are bundles of small ones. Break it apart, then find the vital few.
fSubHint: Brain-dump everything it's made of. You'll prioritise next.
subEmpty: Nothing here yet. List the pieces, then tap a star to mark the ones that matter most.
legend: the vital few — the {count} sub-skills that drive most of the result. Drill these first.
legend_one: the vital few — the {count} sub-skill that drives most of the result. Drill it first.
legend_other: the vital few — the {count} sub-skills that drive most of the result. Drill these first.
p3Cap: Seven principles that separate practice that compounds from practice that stalls. Check each off once you've built it into your plan.
p4Cap: Hours don't count until they're on the page. Log each session — the meter at the top moves with you.
fWhatPh: Drilled the weak point: F-chord transitions
fNoticedHint: Optional — but reflection is where the learning lands.
fNoticedPh: Slowing down made the change cleaner
sessEmpty: No reps logged yet. Even fifteen honest minutes belongs here.
footer: Built on deliberate practice, ultralearning, and the science of retrieval & spacing. Your ledger saves itself as you go.
modalBody: This clears your current target, sub-skills, principles, and every logged session. There's no undo.
p5Cap: Principle 6, made automatic. Add anything worth keeping; it resurfaces on a widening schedule — tomorrow, then days, then weeks.
reviewAddHint: A concept, a phrase, a move — anything you don't want to forget.
reviewEmpty: Nothing queued yet. Add what you want to remember and it'll come back on schedule.
reviewAllClear: All caught up — nothing due today.
chartEmpty: Log a session to start your activity chart.
jsonError: That isn't a valid backup file.
jsonConfirmBody: This replaces everything in your ledger — target, sub-skills, principles, sessions and reviews — with the contents of the backup.
focusHint: Time a real session — it logs straight to your practice log when you're done.
focusGoalReached: Target reached — keep going, or log it.
goalMet: Goal met for this week — nicely done.
celebBody: You've logged {hours} hours toward {skill}. That's the finish line you set.

principles.name: Map the terrain
principles.blurb: Before grinding, spend a little time learning how the skill is structured and how good people actually got good. A short map saves long detours.
principles.prompt: How will you find out the best way to learn this? (a person, a course, a breakdown)

principles.name: Practice the real thing
principles.blurb: Skill transfers poorly. If you want to speak a language, speak; if you want to ship code, ship. Practice in the setting you'll actually use it.
principles.prompt: What does doing the real thing look like for you?

principles.name: Attack the weak point
principles.blurb: Don't just repeat what's comfortable. Isolate the single hardest sub-skill and drill it on purpose, at the edge of what you can do.
principles.prompt: Which sub-skill is your bottleneck right now?

principles.name: Test, don't review
principles.blurb: Re-reading feels productive but barely sticks. Producing the answer from memory is what builds it. Quiz yourself; perform from blank.
principles.prompt: How will you make yourself recall, not just re-read?

principles.name: Shorten the feedback loop
principles.blurb: You can't fix what you can't see. Set up the fastest honest signal you can — a tutor, a test, a recording of yourself, a working/not-working check.
principles.prompt: What will tell you quickly when you're wrong?

principles.name: Space it out
principles.blurb: Cramming fades. Revisit material across days and weeks with growing gaps, and it lodges for good. Schedule the return, don't trust the moment.
principles.prompt: When will you revisit the hard parts again?

principles.name: Push past the plateau
principles.blurb: Once you're competent, the textbook method stops paying off. Invent your own constraints, copy then break the rules, try harder variants.
principles.prompt: What's one experiment to try once the basics feel easy?

confirmDelete: Delete this skill and all its data? This cannot be undone.
pwTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
syncNote: Your data syncs to any device you sign in on.
confirmDeleteAccount: Delete your account and all data permanently? This cannot be undone.
streakStart: Practice today to start a streak
onbBody: Pick something you want to get good at. In four steps you define it, break it down, practise, and review — and watch the mastery meter climb.
aiUnconfigured: AI isn't set up on this server
sampleWhy: Play songs I love at gatherings
shareNote: Anyone with the link can view this skill's progress (read-only).
